title: Podman info has become slower again, when running on ubuntu or debian
body:
### Feature request description

Previously we were able to speed up the execution time of `podman info`, by using `dlocate`

* #19317

But it still takes a long time to execute, and it has gotten longer with 4.9/5.0 compared to 3.4

When using `podman info` (or `podman-remote version`) to test, the delay can be annoying...

### Suggest potential solution

When querying deb packages, containers-common should use "dlocate" for both searches.

It should also **not** try again with dpkg and dpkg-query, if already tried it with dlocate.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

There could be a configuration to hide the packaging information from the output, perhaps

Other container runtimes are using the output from `--version`, rather than the package

### Additional context

It seems that for some reason it is also slowing down podman-remote version? Not only info

`time podman-remote version` takes 5 seconds to execute, while it probably should not do so

comments.body:
When installing `dlocate` and the text database, `podman info` takes less than a second (which is still OK)

With `debfile` and the binary database ([GNU dbm](https://www.gnu.org.ua/software/gdbm/)), the execution time is a fraction of a second (<100 ms)

Each lookup:

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/10364051/b50464e8-1dd1-4572-b6ee-fa51cd144b08)

https://github.com/afbjorklund/debfile

----

The main reason it is slower in Podman v5 compared to v3, is the large number of new / unpackaged helpers...

And that the version code assumes that it is reasonable fast to look up versions, like it is when using `rpm -qf`.

The program `pasta` dumps some license spam in the output of --version, it probably needs filtering out?

```
  pasta:
    executable: /usr/local/libexec/podman/pasta
    package: Unknown
    version: |
      pasta 2024_02_20.1e6f92b
      Copyright Red Hat
      GNU General Public License, version 2 or later
        <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/old-licenses/gpl-2.0.html>
      This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it.
      There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law.
```

when compared with a more relevant (although non-standard)

```
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/local/libexec/podman/slirp4netns
    package: Unknown
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.2.3
      commit: c22fde291bb35b354e6ca44d13be181c76a0a432
      libslirp: 4.1.0
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.1
```

EDIT: separate issue:

* https://github.com/containers/common/issues/1931
Your c/common changes are merged so I assume this can be closed

As long as the new c/c is included in Podman, it should fix the issue

EDIT: Fix is in `v0.58.0-41-g1c6cb2ca`, it has `v0.58.0-40-gbc5f97c3`...

@afbjorklund: Reopened this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22102#issuecomment-2036337446):

>/reopen


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
We always vendor a new version for the next minor release, also https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22243 contains you changes so it will be fixed soon on main

I think I will leave `debfile` as a downstream podman (vendor c/c) patch, we could add it later if anyone actually asks.

```diff
--- a/vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/version/version.go
+++ b/vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/version/version.go
@@ -74,6 +74,7 @@ func Package(program string) string { // program is full path
        }
        packagers := []Packager{
                {"rpm", []string{"/usr/bin/rpm", "-q", "-f"}},
+               {"deb", []string{"/usr/bin/debfile"}},                   // Debian, Ubuntu (fast)
                {"deb", []string{"/usr/bin/dlocate", "-F"}},             // Debian, Ubuntu (quick)
                {"deb", []string{"/usr/bin/dpkg", "-S"}},                // Debian, Ubuntu (slow)
                {"pacman", []string{"/usr/bin/pacman", "-Qo"}},          // Arch
```


Most don't care if it is 1.0 or 0.1 seconds.

But 10 seconds, that is still too annoying...
